source: CT6-6 §5 (SMP-1…5) — the Seeded MemPalace service on the v3.23.0 substrate; authenticated download of a seeded bundle (schema + curated + phenotype catalog + research synthesis) that merges without clobbering the user's projects

scenarios: a re-download refreshes seeded sections but preserves all user projects
acceptance_criteria: defined bundle: schema + curated + phenotype_catalog + research_synthesis (freshness stamp, SMP-5)
merge_into_local refreshes ONLY seeded keys, preserves EVERY user top-level key (SMP-3)

scenarios: only entitled phenotypes carry full records, master is never corrupted
acceptance_criteria: catalog REUSES scripts/phenotypes/discover_phenotypes (not a new schema)
gate_catalog ships full record only for entitled; non-entitled = browse metadata + record None
served records strip internal _dir/_label_dir path fields

scenarios: a forged identity cannot obtain another user's entitlements
acceptance_criteria: client signs the download request via services/common/handshake.py + merges (runs before MemTime)
server verifies the envelope; unsigned/tampered/replayed rejected
entitlements resolved by the VERIFIED public key, NOT the self-asserted requester string (no impersonation)
services/seeded_mempalace stdlib-only core, design-not-deployed; ChromaDB/server/network/billing are adapters; MemTime interpreted + disclosed
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only service + tests).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — ChromaDB, the live seeded-MemPalace HTTP server, the network transport, and the billing system are external infra behind adapter boundaries (not in-repo); the deterministic core (bundle/merge, catalog gate, signed request, handshake verify) is exercised by the pytest suite with an injected transport + in-process Ed25519 signing + the real phenotype store.

confirmed_stubs.why: the real HTTP GET to the project's server is the operator's (the separated tier's own dep, REPO-4); the core injects the transport (the server's handle_bundle_request is the in-process counterpart) — an adapter boundary, not a gap
the SMP-4 purchase/entitlement issuance is an external system; the core injects entitlements_for(requester, public_key) and resolves access by the verified key — documented, not a gap

confirmed_stubs.stub: the seeded MemPalace bundle content + ChromaDB write
confirmed_stubs.why: the real curated content + research synthesis (SMP-5) live in the operator's ChromaDB-backed MemPalace; the core defines the bundle CONTRACT + the non-clobbering merge — by design, not a gap
